Difficulty Limits
fields.definition: Per difficulty values to limit the number of guys in particular behaviours

max berserk count#How many guys in a single clump can be berserking at one time
min berserk count#We'd like at least this number of guys in a single clump can be berserking at one time (primarily combat forms)

cover flags!
hide behind cover time:seconds#how long we stay behind cover after seeking cover
Cover conditions
fields.definition: The following fields dictate the conditions under which we will go into cover.

fields.name: Cover shield fraction#Only cover when shield falls below this level
Cover vitality threshold#Only cover when vitality falls below this level
Disallow cover distance:wus#Disallow covering from visible target closer than this distance
Cover danger threshold#Danger must be this high to cover. At a danger level of 'danger threshold', the chance of seeking cover is the cover chance lower bound (below)
Danger upper threshold#At or above danger level of upper threshold, the chance of seeking cover is the cover chance upper bound (below)
Cover bounds
fields.definition:
The bounds on the chance of seeking cover.
The lower bound is valid when the danger is at 'cover danger threshold'.
The upper bound is valid when the danger is at or above 'danger upper threshold'.
It is interpolated linearly everywhere in between.
 All chances are expressed as 'chance of triggering cover in a 1 second period'.

fields.name: Cover chance#Bounds on the chances of seeking cover, given the above conditions are valid.
Shielded cover bounds
fields.definition: Same as above, but when shielded. If both values are 0 (which is the default for all existing character tags), then the normal values, above, will be used. If you don't want a character to cover when shielded, then set 'cover shield fraction' to 0 instead.

fields.name: Shielded cover chance#Bounds on the chances of seeking cover when shielded, given the above conditions are valid.
Other
Cover check delay:seconds#Amount of time I will wait before trying again after covering (0 value defaults to 2 seconds)
Emerge from cover when shield fraction reaches threshold#Emerge from cover when shield fraction reaches threshold
Proximity self-preserve:wus#Triggers self-preservation when target within this distance (assuming proximity_self_preserve_impulse is enabled)
proximity melee distance#When self preserving from a target less than given distance, causes melee attack (assuming proximity_melee_impulse is enabled)
unreachable enemy danger threshold#When danger from an unreachable enemy surpasses threshold, actor cover (assuming unreachable_enemy_cover impulse is enabled)
scary target threshold#When target is aware of me and surpasses the given scariness, self-preserve (assuming scary_target_cover_impulse is enabled)
Shield depleted cover chance#Probability of going straight into cover when shield is depleted (assuming shield_depleted_cover_impulse is enabled)

Vitality fraction shield equipment{Vitality fraction bubbleshield}#Fraction of vitality below which an equipped shield equipment (instant cover/bubbleshield) will be activated (once damage has died down, and assuming shield_equipment_impulse is enabled)
Recent damage shield equipment#Must have less than this amount of recent body damage before we can deploy our equipped shield equipment.

Shield threshold#When shield vitality drops below given amount, retreat is triggered by low_shield_retreat_impulse
Scary target threshold#When confronting an enemy of over the given scariness, retreat is triggered by scary_target_retreat_impulse

min/max cower timeout bounds#actor times out of cower after the given amount of time
proximity ambush threshold#If target reaches is within the given proximity, an ambush is triggered by the proximity ambush impulse
awareness ambush threshold#If target is less than threshold (0-1) aware of me, an ambush is triggered by the vulnerable enemy ambush impulse
leader dead retreat chance#If leader-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when our leader dies within 4 world units of us
peer dead retreat chance#If peer-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when one of our peers (friend of the same race) dies within 4 world units of us
second peer dead retreat chance#If peer-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when a second peer (friend of the same race) dies within 4 world units of us
flee timeout:seconds#Flee for no longer than this time (if there is no cover, then we will keep fleeing indefinitely). Value of 0 means 'no timeout'
zig-zag angle:degrees#The angle from the intended destination direction that a zig-zag will cause
zig-zag period:seconds#How long it takes to zig left and then zag right.
retreat grenade chance#The likelihood of throwing down a grenade to cover our retreat
backup weapon#If I want to flee and I don't have flee animations with my current weapon, throw it away and try a ...

search time

Investigate
fields.definition: Orphan offset: Offset from the orphan pos along the orphan hint vector that we will investigate to. Positive value means we are predicting where the target is going, negative value means that we would like to stand back a bit and look along the orphan vector.

fields.name: Orphan offset:wu#(0 value will default to 1.8wu)
Minimum offset:wu#Minimum offset from the target point to investigate, otherwise we just use the target point itself. Not entirely sure about the justification for this one...

max presearch time:seconds#Presearch turns off after the given time
Min certainty radius

ZTG
idle pose delay time:seconds#time range for delays between idle poses
Skip Fraction
fields.definition:
For vocalization with priority < react, multiplies the skip fraction in the dialogue globals tag.
For priorities > react, multiplies the vocalization WEIGHT (i.e. the desire of that character to vocalize RELATIVE to other characters in the scene).
Thus if you hurt the arbiter, say, he's still going to yell at you, no matter what, and if you kill somebody and nobody else is there to witness it, he will yell at you.
As always, 0 means speak normally, 1 means skip everything (don't say anything)

fields.name: character skip fraction:[0,1]
look comment time:s#How long does the player look at an AI before the AI responds?
look long comment time:s#How long does the player look at the AI before he responds with his 'long look' comment?

Accuracy
fields.definition:
Parameters control how accuracy changes over the duration of a series of bursts
Accuracy is an analog value between 0 and 1. At zero, the parameters of the first
firing-pattern block is used. At 1, the parameters in the second block is used. In
between, all the values are linearly interpolated

fields.name: normal accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at normal difficulty
normal accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
heroic accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at heroic difficulty
heroic accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
legendary accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at legendary difficulty
legendary accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
firing patterns
weapon melee damage
rate of fire#how many times per second we pull the trigger (zero = continuously held down)
target tracking:[0,1]#how well our bursts track moving targets. 0.0= fire at the position they were standing when we started the burst. 1.0= fire at current position
target leading:[0,1]#how much we lead moving targets. 0.0= no prediction. 1.0= predict completely.
burst geometry
fields.definition:
at the start of every burst we pick a random point near the target to fire at, on either the left or the right side.
the burst origin angle controls whether this error is exactly horizontal or might have some vertical component.

over the course of the burst we move our projectiles back in the opposite direction towards the target. this return motion is also controlled by an angle that specifies how close to the horizontal it is.

for example if the burst origin angle and the burst return angle were both zero, and the return length was the same as the burst length, every burst would start the same amount away from the target (on either the left or right) and move back to exactly over the target at the end of the burst.

fields.name: burst origin radius:world units#how far away from the target the starting point is
burst origin angle:degrees#the range from the horizontal that our starting error can be
burst return length:world units#how far the burst point moves back towards the target (could be negative)
burst return angle:degrees#the range from the horizontal that the return direction can be
burst duration:seconds#how long each burst we fire is
burst separation:seconds#how long we wait between bursts
weapon damage modifier#what fraction of its normal damage our weapon inflicts (zero = no modifier)
projectile error:degrees#error added to every projectile we fire
burst angular velocity:degrees per second#the maximum rate at which we can sweep our fire (zero = unlimited)
maximum error angle:degrees#cap on the maximum angle by which we will miss target (restriction on burst origin radius

Equipment
fields.definition: Create one of these for each equipment type that you would like a character to be able to use. If you specify a relative drop chance of greater than zero then the character will drop this type of equipment with that (relative) chance. If you leave the equipment field empty, then there will be that (relative) chance of dropping no equipment at all.

fields.name: equipment^#The equipment item that is to be usable
flags
relative drop chance#The relative chance of this equipment being dropped with respect to the other pieces of equipment specified in this block
equipment use
